verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytoplasmic heat shock protein (Hsp70). It stabilizes proteins against aggregation and mediates protein folding in the cytosol and organelles; it is also a component of the TIM23 complex (translocase of inner mitochondrial membrane). No TM domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. No documented cell-state-induced ecto-translocation (stress-response Hsp70 upregulation is intracellular, not surface-externalizing); no tissue-restricted surface display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool from vesicular cycling; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. Intracellular chaperone function is the canonical biology. Cytoplasmic resident.
